texto: I might face death any minute now! But I should try not to put myself in harms' way as long as I can live. Of course it is not important if I die, because this is going to happen anyway. I know my purpose, my purpose is: How will my life or death impact the lives of others?
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Samad Behrangi que provoca sentido da existência e experiência vivida. Ao questionar “I might face death any minute now! But I should try not to put myself in harms' way as long as I …”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre educacao, educational, inspiracional, com foco em luto e inspiracao.

frase_original: I might face death any minute now! But I should try not to put myself in harms' way as long as I can live. Of course it is not important if I die, because this is going to happen anyway. I know my purpose, my purpose is: How will my life or death impact the lives of others?

texto: From the will of a freedom-fighter, Farzad Kamangar:"Is it possible to be a teacher and not show the path to the sea to the little fish of the country? Is it possible to carry the heavy burden of being a teacher and be responsible for spreading the seeds of knowledge and still be silent? Is it possible to see the lumps in the throats of the students and witness their thin and malnourished faces and keep quiet? … I cannot imagine witnessing the pain and poverty of the people of this land and fail to give our hearts to the river and the sea, to the roar and the flood.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Samad Behrangi que provoca limites e escolhas conscientes. Ao questionar “From the will of a freedom-fighter, Farzad Kamangar:"Is it possible to be a teacher and not show …”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre amor, freedom, pedagogy, com foco em pedagogy e progressive.
